original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.version: NICE NG98; last updated and reviewed 2023-10-02; minor link update 2025-03
original_registry_entry.population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for adult hearing-difficulty assessment, red-flag referral, otoscopy, audiology, and hearing-support documentation; partial for children and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — initial, sudden, and urgent assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.3.2 — additional symptoms, ear findings, and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.8.1 — audiology assessment, management, and support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
intended_setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
